test_output_summary: 42 tests passed in tests/test_approval_config.py + tests/test_forge_config.py (19 new approval tests + 23 existing forge_config tests, including the updated full round-trip test that now covers the new approval section). Ruff lint check + ruff format check both clean across all four touched files.
implementation_notes: Added ApprovalConfig (Pydantic v2, extra='forbid') in src/forge/config/models.py with two int fields default_wait_seconds (default 300, ge=0) and max_wait_seconds (default 3600, ge=0), plus a model_validator(mode='after') that rejects default_wait_seconds > max_wait_seconds. Wired approval: ApprovalConfig = Field(default_factory=ApprovalConfig) onto ForgeConfig (mirrors the existing fleet/pipeline default_factory pattern). Added module-level constants DEFAULT_APPROVAL_WAIT_SECONDS / DEFAULT_APPROVAL_MAX_WAIT_SECONDS anchored to ASSUM-001/ASSUM-002 of the CGCP assumptions manifest. Documented the ASSUM-003 deferral inline on ApprovalConfig's docstring (ceiling fallback semantics — cancel/escalate/fail-open — explicitly out of scope; deferred to forge-pipeline-config). Updated module docstring to add the no-nats_core/nats-py/langgraph import boundary statement. Re-exported ApprovalConfig from forge.config.__init__. Module continues to import only pathlib, typing, and pydantic. Updated the pre-existing tests/test_forge_config.py round-trip test fixture to include the new approval section so it still passes after ForgeConfig grew the new field. Ruff format also normalised some pre-existing long lines in that test file as part of meeting AC-010.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `ApprovalConfig` Pydantic v2 model defined with two non-negative integer fields:
completion_promises.evidence: ApprovalConfig added to src/forge/config/models.py as a pydantic.BaseModel(extra='forbid') subclass declaring two int fields with ge=0. Verified by tests/test_approval_config.py::TestApprovalConfigShape (test_model_defines_default_wait_seconds, test_model_defines_max_wait_seconds, test_default_wait_seconds_is_int_typed, test_max_wait_seconds_is_int_typed) and AC-004 negative-rejection tests.

default_wait_seconds: int = Field(default=DEFAULT_APPROVAL_WAIT_SECONDS, ge=0, ...) where DEFAULT_APPROVAL_WAIT_SECONDS = 300. Verified by tests/test_approval_config.py::TestApprovalConfigShape::test_default_wait_seconds_default_is_300.

max_wait_seconds: int = Field(default=DEFAULT_APPROVAL_MAX_WAIT_SECONDS, ge=0, ...) where DEFAULT_APPROVAL_MAX_WAIT_SECONDS = 3600. Verified by tests/test_approval_config.py::TestApprovalConfigShape::test_max_wait_seconds_default_is_3600.

Both int fields use Field(ge=0, ...). Negative values raise pydantic.ValidationError. Verified by tests/test_approval_config.py::TestNegativeValuesRejected (test_negative_default_wait_rejected, test_negative_max_wait_rejected) plus the boundary test test_zero_default_wait_accepted confirming ge=0 (not gt=0).

Added @model_validator(mode='after') _validate_default_not_above_max on ApprovalConfig that raises ValueError naming both fields when default_wait_seconds > max_wait_seconds. Verified by tests/test_approval_config.py::TestDefaultNotAboveMax (test_default_above_max_raises, test_default_equal_to_max_accepted, test_canonical_defaults_are_consistent).

ForgeConfig.approval declared as `approval: ApprovalConfig = Field(default_factory=ApprovalConfig)` alongside existing fleet/pipeline default-factory fields. Verified by tests/test_approval_config.py::TestForgeConfigWiresApproval (test_forge_config_has_approval_field, test_minimal_yaml_gets_default_approval, test_default_factory_isolates_instances).

Added two new round-trip tests covering the approval section: test_roundtrip_preserves_approval_section (approval-only) and test_full_roundtrip_with_all_sections (fleet + pipeline + approval + permissions). Both write a YAML file via yaml.safe_dump, reload via yaml.safe_load + ForgeConfig.model_validate, and assert model_dump(mode='json') equals the original. Also updated the pre-existing tests/test_forge_config.py::TestYamlRoundTrip::test_roundtrip_preserves_all_fields fixture to include the approval section so the existing full round-trip continues to pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Inline comment documents that ASSUM-003 (ceiling fallback semantics) is deferred to `forge-pipeline-config`
completion_promises.evidence: ApprovalConfig docstring contains a dedicated 'Note (ASSUM-003 deferral)' paragraph stating that the terminal behaviour at max_wait_seconds (cancel / escalate / fail-open) is explicitly out of scope and deferred to forge-pipeline-config; max_wait_seconds field description repeats the deferral pointer. Verified by tests/test_approval_config.py::TestAssum003DeferralDocumented::test_docstring_mentions_assum_003_deferral.

src/forge/config/models.py imports only `pathlib.Path`, `typing.Any`, and `pydantic` (BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, field_validator, model_validator). Module docstring records the boundary rule explicitly. Verified by tests/test_approval_config.py::TestNoForbiddenImports::test_module_source_does_not_import_forbidden_packages, which scans the module source for any `import` / `from` statement targeting nats_core, nats_py, nats-py, or langgraph.
